vulnerability.reference: Benchmark datasets like DRCT-2M exhibit texture biases that fail to capture real-world image variability, overestimating robustness.
vulnerability.generated: Benchmark-driven evaluation relies on datasets that omit genuine images with high-frequency textures mimicking diffusion residuals, causing brittle classification boundaries.

impact.reference: Models deployed based on biased benchmarks fail in real settings, wasting resources and exposing users to undetected synthetic media.
impact.generated: Overestimated generalizability leads to real-world detector failures, misclassifying authentic media as generated and undermining forensic trust.

dose_response.reference: Reconstruction-error-based detection models overfit to dataset artifacts, leading researchers to overestimate robustness, causing failures in real-world deployment, wasting resources and exposing users to undetected synthetic media.
dose_response.generated: Reconstruction-error-based detection misclassifies genuine images with diffusion-mimicking textures, and through evaluation on benchmarks that omit such textures, triggers brittle boundaries, leading to overestimated generalizability and real-world failures that misclassify authentic media and undermine forensic trust.
